examples: His spade hits the earth marking only a stunted notch in the desiccated soil.
The old sailor's skin has desiccated from years of being out in the sun and the wind.
Over the years, as Naipaul's fame grew along with his irascibility, the marriage desiccated.
But gone with the desiccated rivers was her link to Dreamtime and her ancestors, .
Nor are they desiccated calculating machines, meting out dispassionate justice uninfluenced by political ideas.
